body:
Bylaw No. 14765 
Page 1 of 3 
 
 
CITY OF BURNABY 
 
 
BYLAW NO. 14765 
 
 
A BYLAW to close and remove the dedication of  
a certain portion of highway 
 
 
WHEREAS pursuant to section 40 of the Community Charter and section 120 of the Land 
Title Act, Council may by bylaw close part of a highway to traffic and remove the dedication 
of that part of the highway; 
 
AND WHEREAS Council, before adopting this Bylaw, has caused the required statutory 
public notice of its intention to be given by advertisement; 
 
AND WHEREAS Council deems it expedient and in the public interest to stop up and close 
to traffic and remove the dedication of that portion of highway more particularly hereinafter 
described, 
 
NOW THEREFORE the Council of the City of Burnaby ENACTS as follows: 
 
1. 
This Bylaw may be cited as BURNABY HIGHWAY CLOSURE BYLAW  
NO. 3, 2025. 
 
2. 
ALL AND SINGULAR those certain parcels or tracts of land, situate, lying and 
being in the City of Burnaby, in the Province of British Columbia, more particularly 
known and described as those portions of road and lane dedicated by Plan 1543 
adjacent to Lot 9, Block 54, District Lot 123, Group 1, New Westminster District, 
Plan 1543 and Lot Q, District Lot 123, Group 1, New Westminster District 
Plan 14906, containing 306.6 m2  and 92.9 m2 shown as Parcel “A” and Parcel “B”, 
respectively, and outlined in bold on Reference Plan EPP148900 (hereinafter called 
“the closed portions of road”), prepared by D. Aaron Creamer, B.C.L.S., a copy of 
which is hereunto annexed, is hereby closed to all types of traffic. 

Bylaw No. 14765 
Page 2 of 3 
 
 
 
3. 
The dedication of the closed portions of road as highway are removed. 
 
Read a first time this 28th day of October, 2025 
 
Read a second time this 28th day of October, 2025 
 
Read a third time this 28th day of October, 2025 
 
Public Notice Dates this 6th day of November, 2025 and 13th day of November, 2025 
 
Reconsidered and adopted this 25th day of November, 2025 
 
 
 
 
 
MAYOR 
 
 
 
 
 
DEPUTY CORPORATE OFFICER 
 
Signed Original Bylaw on file. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

Bylaw No. 14765 
Page 3 of 3

minutes_briefs.title: Burnaby closes two small road portions to traffic
minutes_briefs.summary: The City of Burnaby has passed a bylaw (a local law) to close two small sections of road and lane to all traffic and remove them as public highways. This means those pieces of land are no longer public roads. The closures affect specific parcels near Lot 9, Block 54, District Lot 123, Plan 1543 and Lot Q, Plan 14906, totaling about 399.5 square metres. The bylaw was adopted on November 25, 2025, after public notice was given.
